title: I, you, he, she + 'to be' (ser)
objectives: Conjugate 'ser' (to be) in the present tense
grammar_notes: Spanish usually DROPS the pronoun — the verb ending already tells you who. 'Soy de Rusia' is more natural than 'Yo soy de Rusia'.
Russian also often drops 'я' / 'ты' in casual speech — same intuition.
'ser' is one of two 'to be' verbs in Spanish. The other is 'estar' (lesson 7). For now: ser = who/what you ARE.
example_phrases: ¿Eres ruso? — Sí, soy rusa. (Are you Russian? — Yes, I'm Russian [female].)
